content:
## When the Notice Window Starts
A debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer error notice must be tied to the statement on which the disputed activity appeared. Northstar Bank uses a statement notice window of 60 days after that statement is made available. If the customer is asking whether a dispute can still be reviewed, anchor the answer to the statement date, not the transaction date alone.

## Liability for Unauthorized Activity
The liability rule applies only to unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud. For those categories, a customer who reports the issue within 2 business days after discovering it has maximum liability of $50. If the same customer reports after that discovery period but still within the statement notice window, maximum liability is $500. If the report is after the statement notice window, the benchmark policy treats the exposure as unlimited liability in this benchmark policy.

## Non-Unauthorized Errors
For debit errors that are not unauthorized activity, customer liability is 0. Do not apply the unauthorized-activity liability caps to those cases. See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" for the broader notice rules and "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" when staff need intake steps.
title: Debit Dispute Timing FAQ
